name: Nathan Braner
title: Principal Senior Engineer
tagline: Data-Driven Product Strategist • Cloud Solutions Expert • Full Stack Developer
summary: Methodical technical lead with 8+ years of experience as a software engineer and PHP, backend, and full stack developer. Specific expertise in scalability as it pertains to technical requirements and code writing for the startup B2B retail and e-commerce industries. Track record of guiding high-performing teams through priority product sprints and solutions-focused agile environments.
highlights: Lead teams of 9+ to deliver continuous deployment with low failure rates and quick time frames
8+ years developing scalable web applications using Laravel and Vue.js
Adept in OOP/code planning and TDD testing methodologies
Transitioned several teams to agile environments, including during a complex AWS/GCP migration
Increased data pipeline speed by hours during strategic technical modernization initiative
Steering company-wide AI strategy from conversational interfaces to fully autonomous agentic systems

vision.intro: When applying the core differences between strategy and planning to building an AI app, the insights shift the focus from simply "writing code" to creating a product that actually dominates its market.

pillars.title: Avoid the "Comfort Trap" of Building AI Features
pillars.content: It is incredibly easy to fall into the planning trap—choosing models, building UIs, hiring developers. Because you control these costs, they feel comfortable. But a list of impressive AI features is not a strategy; it is just a plan. True strategy requires specifying a competitive outcome where customers want your product enough to make it profitable. You control the code, but you don't control users—they decide if your app succeeds.

pillars.title: Find Your App's Niche
pillars.content: Many developers build generic AI tools, which is just participating without a theory of how to win. Instead of doing everything, you need an integrative set of choices that positions your app on a specific playing field where you can dominate. Like Southwest Airlines chose to compete with Greyhound by offering cheap, point-to-point flights—align everything to that goal. Build your entire technical architecture to solve one problem better than anyone else.

pillars.title: Embrace the "Angst" of Unproven AI
pillars.content: If your app's roadmap feels entirely safe, you are likely guaranteeing a loss. Strategy has angst because you cannot prove in advance it will succeed. Accept that your theory on how the market will react cannot be perfect. Taking that leap is not bad management; it is great leadership that gives your app a chance to achieve something extraordinary.

pillars.title: Build a Testable, Single-Page AI Strategy
pillars.content: A great strategy can be written on a single page: Where are you choosing to play? How are you choosing to win? What capabilities do you need? Then ask: "What would have to be true about ourselves, the industry, the competition, and the customers for this strategy to work?" Launch, watch real users interact, and continuously hone your strategy as the world unfolds.
